Reporting Position in Ohio

May 1991
Investigative MethodsJournalism & Newsroom

The Dayton Daily News is hiring a projects reporter with investigative skills and computer-assisted reporting expertise, requiring applicants to submit resumes and clips to the listed address.

The Dayton Daily News is accepting applications for a projects reporting position. Candidates must have demonstrated investigative reporting skills and an aptitude for using personal computers as a reporting tool. In the past year we have done computer-assisted reporting projects on discrimination in court sentencing, campaign finance and safety in the workplace. We have sent reporters through courses at the MICAR and have worked with faculty members of the National Institute for Advanced Reporting at Indiana University.

Applicants should send resumes and clips to Jim Ripley, executive metro editor at: Dayton Daily News Fourth and Ludlow Streets Dayton, OH 45402
